reasoning: The resolution threshold requires roughly 74 ACLED-coded fatalities in a single 30-day window (> 10 * 7.333). Based on the stated baseline, the 30-day average over the prior 360 days was only 6.33, indicating that ACLED's strict coding for Ecuador only captured ~76 fatalities over the entire prior year. Thus, resolving YES means achieving an entire year's worth of ACLED-recorded fatalities in just 30 days. Normally, this base rate is extremely low (<5%). However, March 2025 is seeing severe escalations ahead of the April 13 presidential runoff between Noboa and González. A single gang shootout in Guayaquil on March 7 killed 22 people (~30% of the threshold), and President Noboa has publicly granted preemptive pardons for military and police to conduct extreme crackdowns. This creates a catalytic environment where state-vs-gang clashes could easily hit the 74-fatality mark in late March and April. Therefore, I estimate a 15-20% chance for the April dates, which cover this peak election violence window and the recent massacres. Following the instruction to increase uncertainty (move toward 0.5) for more distant horizons, the June and September probabilities are gradually increased.
